11.content: Lord Tamara had already downed four bottles of whiskey, and was halfway through his eighth one. He was still in control of his senses and he knew he should stop, but he simply couldn't. Every year, this was the hardest day for him. On this day, 12th September, nine years ago, his wife, his one and only true love had died.
11.title: Tamara Lord

Tomas goes to check on their children
Someone calls Tomas over
Something else
114.content:
NINE YEARS EARLIER:

The annual Guild ball was off to a roaring start, as per usual. How could it not be? The event had been planned meticulously two years beforehand to accommodate any and all persons that would be attending by an extensive team of a thousand elves that had been worked to the very bone planning all types of decorations and catering whilst also prepping a multitude of mesmers and hexes to protect the prestigious night from unwanted eyes. Drinks were flowing, a quartet of Sirens sang smooth jazz over the scintillating piano melody of a dreadlocked Hecatoncheires and the floor was bustling with eloquent folk, all more than eager to chitchat about everything and nothing at all.

Ah, if only Tomas had not been groomed from birth to sit in the same seat year in, year out, and could enjoy this event and all its grandeur for the first time once again. It had barely been a half hour and he was already well into his seventh Martini, downing the drink with ease before rolling the olive with his tongue to drain the rest. “Another,” he hollered to the orc waitress strolling past nearby. The waitress obliged and passed him a fresh glass poured to the brim. He nodded to her and she batted her eyelids before pirouetting with surprise grace for such a lumbering creature and strutted onto the next table. Tomas heard a click of a tongue coming from beside him. He smirked as he looked towards his wife, Tamara, sitting opposite him on the table with an exasperated expression plastered on her face. She cleaned up nice, Tomas remarked to himself. She’d pristinely and precisely applied her makeup, adding a dash of purple shade to her eyelids and splashing a lick of black eyeliner to boot. She’d coiffed her hair up into a regal bun, allowing her catlike hazel irises to glow and light her face without fear of a stray lock of black hair curling into them, and touched up her lips very nicely, carefully laying on a coat of blood red lipstick that made the hair on the back of Tomas’ neck crackle with lust.

“You’re staring again, Tomas.” She hadn’t even looked up. Tomas playfully darted his eyes away, seeing Tamara smirk in the corner of his eye. “Fine you caught me.” He leaned forward in his seat and whispered, “I must say I am loving this look of yours, my wife. You look not a day older than thirty, my love. Might I ask why you don’t spoil me with this wardrobe everyday?”

Tamara scoffed. “I love you, Tomas, but if you believe I would even attempt to balance my housework, cooking, cleaning, looking after the girls, all whilst making sure you are satisfied and content, with adorning myself in this elaborate getup you are sadly mistaken.”

Tomas sarcastically rolled his eyes. “Whatever, Mother,” he groaned. Tamara slapped his leg playfully and chortled. “Don’t say that,” she said, “you make me sound like an old crone.”

“Your feelings do not make it nonetheless true.”

“Your saying it being nonetheless true makes it no less unflattering. Ah, the prices we pay to keep the Bloodlines pure.”

“If we are talking purity then I must make a comment on your outfit.” Tomas took Tamara’s hand in his own and kissed it softly. “I must say you must be the loveliest damsel in this room tonight. May I ask who it is you are trying to impress and how long you’ve been sleeping with them?”

“Tomas!” She giggled and smacked his hand away. “Hush! Your careless whispers may yet lead to my suitor’s undoing!”

“Then hurry, woman, time is of the essence if I am to destroy his social standing!” They laughed together. Tomas’ eyes strayed again once more and he smiled as his eyes came upon a figure standing in the midst of some elegantly dressed officials. These officials were obviously of much importance in the machinations of the Guild judging by their ornate garments and bloated ego. The man standing between them though was a sharp contrast; his clothes, though equally smart as anything Tomas would’ve worn on one of his best days, was understated and quite plain, a dark black suit with nearly imperceptible grey pinstripes. His shoulders were small and thin but his chest was thrust triumphantly outwards, almost laughably so if Tomas were the type of man to laugh. But strangely (if you could call it strange in the midst of all this chaos) was his most bizarre and discerning feature; atop the scrawny build and his pale neck was the beautiful visage of an African woman, with his long hair curled into an Afro over one eye and possessing bulbous glossed lips that could make even the staunchest heterosexual man begin to crave kissing them. Word of its origins were rampant around the guild and because none of them were directly confirmed by the man himself they continued to circulate until they became a tired subject of conversation. Personally Tomas was leaning more into the Ugandan hussie narrative but with Dullahan as reclusive as the great Raser Bunnor, one could never really be certain.

Tomas watched as Raser turned around during one of his conversations and looked his direction. Their eyes locked and Raser waved gleefully. Tomas raised his glass in return. Tamara noticed who Tomas was looking at and, once she recognised him, sighed and covered her face sheepishly. Tomas laughed. “If it’s who I think it is,” he said, taunting her, “I may have to seize my opportunity to confront him for your affections swiftly.”

“Don’t bother, my love, I’d rather bed a frog rather than take him as my partner.” A hint of realisation creeped across Tamara’s face. “Wait, what’s the rush?”

Tomas ran his finger round the rim of his glass and said, “If I am to believe his word, he is going to be announced an Enforcer tonight. Raser will finally be given the job he’s desired since we were just younglings in the Guild’s training program and be christened the Fanidiego, overseer of all the minor Guild military operations this side of the ocean. He sure seemed hellbent on making a big farce of telling me beforehand over the phone...”

“Hah! The power of nepotism strikes once more! I’ve never liked that boy: too handsome and too arrogant for his own good. I tried so hard to keep him as far away from you as possible as you grew and matured but he always seemed to come crawling back. You know, your father and I could’ve used the respect our Bloodline has gained over countless generations of loyal service to the Guild to forcibly seize power but we chose not to, instead choosing the simpler life outside of the Guild’s watchful eyes where we were free to live and raise our family in the manner we wanted to.”

Tamara took a moment to read Tomas. His eyes seemed to be storming over. “Something the matter, Tomas?”

Tomas shrugged with passive-aggression and called for yet another glass. “Maybe you should’ve. Better title, better pay...”

“What’s mortal money to ones like us? Please, don’t let these worldly pursuits consume your mind, you’re better than that.
114.title: She was killed by Fanidiego, and the fact Tamar...

13.content:
Tina, the eldest, who seemed to be the most innocent one out of the three, held a dark side that not many knew. She'd slip out in the middle of the night, go to pubs with her fake ID, pretend to get wasted just to sleep with any guy she thinks looks like her type. The clothes that her parents buy were completely different than what she wears when she goes out every single night, just so no one recognises her.

One day, unfortunately or not, while she's trying to get out from the window, her father Tomas, comes into her room just to find her not in bed but trying to go out with sexy clothes. He grabs her arm, pulls her in and whisper's in her ear, "Have been a bad girl to daddy, baby?"
13.title: Tina Lord

choices: Saying this, he sealed the proposal with a kiss on the lips.
Saying this, he kissed you on your and waited for you to gather your wits.
You exclaimed in complete surprise,"But I never thought of you like that."
"I have always loved you too, Ardex.", saying this you both kissed.
"I don't know what to say, Ardex."
14.content:
Tatiana hated Tilly from the bottom of her heart. Somehow, though she had the better name, Tilly excelled at everything else! She got more dates, topped every class, has more friends, and was everybody's favorite. Even her father, Lord Tamara seemed to like her more. The only person who cared more about Tatiana than Tilly was late Lady Cinniana, the Twins' mother. Tatiana kept complaining about all this to her childhood best friend, Ardex. She felt invisible in her presence.

One morning, just like any other, Tatiana was ranting and ranting about Tilly to Ardex, when suddenly he said,"I don't know how you haven't noticed this before, but there is this one person I know who certainly will always prioritize you more than Tilly."
"Not possible. Everyone loves that bitch."
"But you have been to oblivious to notice that I have always loved you. And Tilly, or anybody for that matter, cannot change how I feel about you, Nia."
14.title: Tatiana Lord

choices: "Nia, get out. Ardex & I want to talk." she said, smiling seductively.
Tilly had been in love with him for a very long time now.
"I'm sorry, am I interrupting something?", she asked politely.
"Nia, Dad's wasted again. We gotta help him."
"Tilly, get out. We're in the middle of something."
142.content:
"Ardex!", she cried, standing up quickly.
"Too soon?", he backed up, clearly frightened by her reaction.
"What do you think you are doing?"
"I'm sorry. I really am. Can we just put this behind and continue on like nothing ever happened? Please?"
"No. No, we can't. I'm sorry. I overreacted. But we can't just continue on like nothing happened."
"So what do you want to do now?"
Tatiana looked at his perplexed hazel eyes. They were so deep, she often imagined herself falling into them. His brown curls stuck out in all directions, framing his gentle face in shabbily. 'It's just Ardex. Stop freaking out. You know him so well. Calm down.', she said to herself, calming down enough to sit beside him again. Color returned to his pale face.
"Listen, the thing is-"
"Ardex! I didn't know you were here!", squealed Tilly, barging into her sister's room without knocking, as usual.
"Oh, Tilly. Hi."said Ardex.
142.title: Saying this, he kissed you on your and waited f...

145.content:
To be quite frank Tatiana liked Ardex too, but doubts and insecurity swarmed through her mind as she thought "What if he's only dong this to get close to Tilly?" Little she knew that the boy was genuinely in love with her and his heart broke a little when she said "Are you sure it's not Tilly?" He looked at her in shock and worry.

"No Nia, it's always been you. The way you are close to me and that you trust me tells me that you like me too. You have been vulnerable to me as I have been to you."

"But that's nothing Ardex, many friends have been vulnerable to each other and yet remain friends."

He had enough and slid his hand at the back of her neck and put his lips on top of hers to prove to her that its her he loves, its her he dreams of every night, its her he wishes to spend the rest of their life with.

But they don't know is that Tilly was watching them from afar and jealousy sprang from her as she had crushed on Ardex for as long as she can remember.
145.title: "I don't know what to say, Ardex."

choices: Ardex says,"Nia isn't any of those things! She's Beautiful and deserves better!"
You fight Tilly verbally.
You break up with Ardex.
Everyone or a few people speak up for Ardex and your love.
You run away into the darkness.
1451.content:
I never felt happier in her life till now as she's kissing Ardex. I forgot all about Tilly, about how nobody cares about me, and every other trouble in the world. However, it all ends when Tilly screams, "How dare you!" She points in my face and says, "How dare you dirty the man I love."

Everyone looks in my direction. Before I can respond to her bitchy face, Ardex pushes me behind him and says, "Leave her alone. She hasn't done anything. I was the one who kissed her."

Tilly starts tearing up and says, "What do you mean? You don't love me?"

"No, I don't." He says.

"But, I loved you since the day we met, how can you not love me?" She asks, sobbing.

"I loved someone else. Someone who was forced to live in the dark. Someone who has caught my eye and heart." Ardex said as he looked at Tatiana.

"So you love Tatiana? That ugly good for nothing of a sister?" Tilly asked in despise.
1451.title: Tatiana kisses back and accepts the proposal.

15.content:
Tilly was amazing. She was born amazing. Everyone loved her, worshiped her, and wanted to experience her presence. Everyone except her stupid, useless twin sister Titiana. No one even cared about Titiania and yet she had the audacity to disrespect Tilly. Tilly felt like Titiana took her for granted simply because they share a room. That ignorant idiot. 'Oh, well', she thought, 'her loss'. Until she realized that the she had a huge crush in Titiania, childhood best friend, Ardex.

She never understood why Ardex liked to hang out with that moron in comparision to an absolute stunner like her. She tried so hard so constantly to make Ardex look at her, even for a second, but in vain.
15.title: Tilly the lord
